rejected_message.content: {"strong_anchor":{"grounding":4,"commercial_judgement":4,"scepticism":4,"european_context":3,"output_usefulness":4,"rationale":"Correctly recomputes NRR from the D02 bridge ((17.25-1.95+2.95)/17.25=105.8%) to directly rebut MC01, and ties MC02's 'all recurring and deployed' claim to the D08 EUR1.30m non-live/invoiced overhang. Cash-quality challenge is grounded in D06 (capitalised dev EUR2.10m vs EUR0.70m prior year, excluded EUR1.40m SWAT payroll, cash from ops EUR2.00m vs EUR2.10m capex outflow) without overstating the exact normalised EBITDA figure, which is appropriately cautious. Concentration/renewal risk correctly cites D03/D07 (Customer A 12.4% of ARR, nine-month retender, acquired-site roll-in). The one gap is that MC04's inflated EUR8.5bn TAM versus the D10 bottom-up build is never interrogated, and the DACH reseller specifics (90-day notice, incomplete audit templates in D09) are referenced but not quantified. The HOLD framing with explicit gating diligence actions (A01-A04) is well matched to the actual decision point (authorise/reject confirmatory diligence) and is highly actionable."},"weak_anchor":{"grounding":0,"commercial_judgement":0,"scepticism":0,"european_context":1,"output_usefulness":1,"rationale":"Recites management's headline NRR, margin, growth and TAM figures verbatim without citing a single evidence ID and without acknowledging any of the contradictions in the packet (D02/D08 ARR definition mismatch, D06 EBITDA quality issues, D07 concentration, D09 re
